keyspaces.description: Register each llm-worker worker under /services/llm-worker/ as a leased key (value host:port) with a TTL keepalive, so listeners discover the live worker set.

history.description: Register each llm-worker worker under /services/llm-worker/ as a leased key (value host:port) with a TTL keepalive, so listeners discover the live worker set.

listeners.description: Watch /services/llm-worker/ and keep a live in-memory map of llm-worker workers (worker id -> host:port), updated by pushed etcd events. rebalance the mapping of usr-msg-consumer to llm-worker(s) using WORKER_RATIO from config/app-settings as a guide. there should be max WORKER_RATIO llm workers per usr-msg-consumer. when either the llm-workers, usr-msg-consumers, or WORKER_RATIO update the new assignments should be sent to the llm-workers via UpdateConsumer and sent to usr-msg-consumers via UpdateWorkers. during rebalancing, llm workers should stay connected to their same usr-msg-consumer if possible. if they have no current usr-message-consumer then least-connections should be used to assign.

history.description: Watch /services/llm-worker/ and keep a live in-memory map of llm-worker workers (worker id -> host:port), updated by pushed etcd events.

keyspaces.name: app-settings
keyspaces.description: Config keyspace /config/app-settings/ — key/value settings pushed live to watching services.

history.description: Config keyspace /config/app-settings/ — key/value settings pushed live to watching services.

listeners.description: Watch /config/app-settings/ and keep a live in-memory config map (key -> value), updated by pushed etcd events. rebalance the mapping of usr-msg-consumer to llm-worker(s) using WORKER_RATIO from config/app-settings as a guide. there should be max WORKER_RATIO llm workers per usr-msg-consumer. when either the llm-workers, usr-msg-consumers, or WORKER_RATIO update the new assignments should be sent to the llm-workers via UpdateConsumer and sent to usr-msg-consumers via UpdateWorkers. during rebalancing, llm workers should stay connected to their same usr-msg-consumer if possible. if they have no current usr-message-consumer then least-connections should be used to assign.

history.description: Watch /config/app-settings/ and keep a live in-memory config map (key -> value), updated by pushed etcd events.

Watch /config/app-settings/ and keep a live in-memory config map (key -> value), updated by pushed etcd events. rebalance the mapping of usr-msg-consumer to llm-worker(s) using WORKER_RATIO from config/app-settings as a guide. there should be max WORKER_RATIO llm workers per usr-msg-consumer. when either the llm-workers, usr-msg-consumers, or WORKER_RATIO update the new assignments should be sent to the llm-workers via UpdateConsumer and sent to usr-msg-consumers via UpdateWorkers. during rebalancing, llm workers should stay connected to their same usr-msg-consumer if possible. if they have no current usr-message-consumer then least-connections should be used to assign.

listeners.description: Watch /config/app-settings/ and keep a live in-memory config map (key -> value), updated by pushed etcd events. Read CONFIG['LAST_TOKEN'] (default "DONE") at token-emit time and stream it, in place of token id 26, as the END marker of the redis token stream tokens:<id> (whose values are the a-z characters of each generated token).

history.description: Watch /config/app-settings/ and keep a live config map; read LAST_TOKEN (default "DONE") as the redis token stream's END marker.
